headline: Sabah demands return of 40% oil royalty rights
context: Sabah Chief Minister Hajiji Noor formally requested renegotiation of the Malaysia Agreement 1963 petroleum terms, seeking 40% royalty up from the current 5%.

cards.big: Sabah wants 40% oil royalty — eight times the current 5% share
cards.sub: Chief Minister cites MA63 original spirit and Sarawak's improved 2024 deal

cards.big: MA63 review committee has met only 3 times since 2020 formation
cards.sub: Federal government repeatedly postponed scheduled sessions citing 'scheduling conflicts'

cards.big: Sabah contributed RM48 billion in petroleum revenue since 2015
cards.sub: State received only RM2.4 billion back — a 95% federal retention rate

cards.big: Sarawak secured additional autonomy in 7 areas under GPS agreement
cards.sub: Sabah's BN-led government lacks comparable bargaining leverage in unity coalition

cards.big: Oil royalty isn't charity — it's a constitutional bargain from 1963 that remains unfulfilled
Can federalism survive if Borneo states feel perpetually shortchanged?
